title: “Yeah but …”: shifting our mindset around body weight in organizational research
journal: Equality, Diversity and Inclusion: An International Journal
abstract: Purpose This manuscript aims to expose a modern inner dialog between antifat beliefs and fat-inclusive beliefs, seeded by literature that sometimes supports the vagaries of excess body weight and sometimes points to other causes and neutral consequences of excess body weight. It shows how we, as researchers, “hear” this dialog in our own minds as we attempt to be weight-inclusive in our research on body weight in the workplace. Findings By being transparent in revealing our inner dialog about excess body weight’s harm – and lack thereof – we point to how to overcome some prejudicial beliefs about body weight that have long been entrained by Western culture. Research limitations/implications Showing that there are (at least) two parallel arguments about fatness – and concluding with a reframe to reconcile the two streams – will help researchers approach weight at work research with more awareness and sensitivity. Further, allowing other researchers to view our inner dialog provides an exemplar about how openness and conversation about how our own biases can come to inform our research and the concrete steps we must take to avoid this. Originality/value It is rare for research on weight to provide two viewpoints: that fatness can be aversive but also that fatness cannot lead to all the maladies we typically associate with it.

title: Work–life balance in the higher education sector: A systematic literature review
journal: SA Journal of Human Resource Management
abstract: Orientation: Academics in higher education (HE) are struggling to strike a balance between work and family life because of excessive work demands, as well as family life pressures. Research purpose: The aim of the study was to provide a comprehensive synthesis and critical evaluation of the literature that currently exists regarding academics’ work–life balance (WLB) within higher education institutions, as well as providing an evidence-based state of knowledge on the subject, major themes, contradictions or research gaps. Motivation for the study: The motivation for this study stems from the growing emphasis on WLB in HE, where faculty members face distinct challenges and demands. Research approach/design and method: The literature review focused on the literature in HE through reviewed articles from 2000 to 2025. The review selected a sample of 112 journal articles from databases that included Web of Science, Directory of Open Access Journals, ScienceDirect, Education Resources Information Centre, PsycINFO and Scopus that focused on faculty or academics in HE. Main findings: The results of this review are summarised in four thematic clusters: the antecedents of imbalance, the psychological and organisational consequences, the intervention strategies and the contextual moderators. The majority of the studies were based in Western countries and fewer from the Global South. Practical/managerial implications: Higher education institutions need to consider moving away from generic policies to provide tailored interventions. They should consider developing flexible work arrangements that are co-designed with employees, and create robust, context-sensitive institutional support systems. Contribution/value-add: An integrated conceptual framework that outlines the interconnection of Border, Conflict, and Zedeck and Mosier’s model, and contemporary drivers of imbalance. Comparative studies and the design of WLB frameworks for academics working in developing economies are necessary.

title: Women in Male-Dominated Fields: The Perceived Glass Ceiling of Women Working in Different Job Collars
abstract: This study investigated the perceived glass ceiling among women in male-dominated fields who are working in blue-, gray-, and white-collar occupations in the Philippines. Glass ceiling refers to the invisible barrier that limits qualified individuals, particularly women, from rising to leadership roles regardless of their skills, qualifications, and achievements. The researchers used a causal-comparative design to determine the level of perceived glass ceiling measured by Glass Ceiling Scale among these occupational groups and to find whether significant differences existed between these groups. Results showed the different levels of the perceived glass ceiling across different job collars: women in blue-collar reported moderate perceived barriers (M=4.09; SD=0.51), white-collar reported slightly low perceived barriers (M=2.76; SD=0.59), while gray-collar workers reported low perceived barriers (M=2.60; SD=0.65). Using Welch’s ANOVA, results revealed that there is a significant difference in glass ceiling perceptions across job collars (p=0.001). To further explore the significant differences, a Games-Howell post hoc test was conducted to identify which specific pairs of job collar types differ significantly in their perceived glass ceiling scores, showing that there is a statistically significant difference between blue-collar and white- and gray-collar women. Meanwhile, there is no significant difference between gray- and white-collar women. These findings highlight the importance of contextualizing gender disparities within occupational structures and highlight the need for collar-specific interventions to promote gender equity in the workplace.

title: Utilization of wild bush passion fruit in making yogurt and ice cream
journal: International Journal of Tourism, Hospitality, and Sustainable Development (IJTHSD)

title: The impact of the digital economy on the sustainable development of the logistics industry in China's Unified National Market
journal: Asia Pacific Journal of Marketing and Logistics
abstract: Purpose This paper aims to study the impact mechanism and internal mechanism of China's digital economy on the sustainable development of the logistics industry, accelerate the digitalization process of China's logistics industry, and ultimately achieve the sustainable development of the logistics industry. Design/methodology/approach Firstly, based on provincial panel data from China, using the entropy method to measure the level of sustainable development of the digital economy and the logistics industry. Secondly, controlling for the provincial fixed effect model and the time fixed effect, a two-way fixed effect model is constructed to empirically test the mechanism of the digital economy's impact on the sustainable development of the logistics industry. Findings The digital economy exerts a substantial influence on the advancement of the sustainable development of logistics industry, the unified national market exerts a positive moderating effect between the digital economy and the sustainable development of the logistics industry, the capacity of green technological innovation and the efficiency of green technological innovation provide green power for the sustainable development of the logistics industry, and the digital economy has a marginal incremental empowering effect on the sustainable development of the logistics industry, additionally, it also exhibits strong spatio-temporal heterogeneity. Research limitations/implications Currently research literature on the impact of the digital economy on the sustainable development of the logistics industry remains limited, with insufficient exploration of relevant theories and influencing mechanisms. Furthermore, certain indicators within the framework constructed in this study may not directly reflect the core environmental or social performance of the industry. Future research should focus on developing more direct and specific evaluation indicator systems to measure the direct effectiveness of sustainable development in the logistics sector, while also incorporating other variables which will enable deeper investigation into the influence of the digital economy on the sustainable development of the logistics industry. Practical implications This paper elucidates the theoretical underpinnings of the digital economy and offers empirical substantiation for the formulation of a contemporary logistics system in the context of the unified national market. The framework exhibits a level of representativeness and methodological soundness. Social implications A sustainable logistics system that is efficient, green, and accessible both internally and externally is of great significance for the logistics industry to reduce costs and increase efficiency, accelerate the construction of a unified national market in China, and promote sustainable economic development. Originality/value First, this study brings the concept of China's unified national market into the research on the sustainable development of China's logistics industry in the digital economy, providing a new research perspective on the dynamic evolution mechanism of this relationship from a quantitative angle. Second, the intermediary research mechanism expands the scope of research on the sustainable development of the logistics industry by examining two dimensions: green technological innovation capacity and green technological innovation efficiency. Finally, this paper further introduces industrial structure upgrading as a threshold variable to explore potential nonlinear mechanisms. Specifically, this paper establishes a comprehensive research framework for the sustainable development of the logistics industry.

title: THE THEORETICAL FOUNDATIONS OF FUTURE DIRECTIONS OF ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE IN HUMAN RESOURCE MANAGEMENT-A SYSTEMATIC LITERATURE REVIEW
journal: EPRA International Journal of Economics Business and Management Studies
abstract: The evolving role of human resource management along side artificial intelligence is explored in this study. Organizations are more inclined towards emphasizing sustainability through long-term retention of employees in this era of digital transformation. Artificial intelligence and machine learning have subsumed almost all human resource functions which mandates this study further. A total of 86 articles were extracted from Scopus database and Biblioshiny along with VOS viewer software was employed to provide a comprehensive overview of the existing literature on this subject. The findings revealed four major clusters that collectively represent the current body of knowledge, further supported by the analysis conducted through Biblioshiny. The study has also proposed an A-M-M-O framework that categorizes the mediators, moderators, and outcomes identified through cluster analysis. Furthermore, the research identifies a significant gap in theoretical foundations and the absence of a comprehensive conceptual framework explaining the integration of artificial intelligence into HR functions. To address this gap, the study establishes the relevance of artificial intelligence in human resource management through the application of economic and sociological perspectives, including Human Capital Theory, Social Exchange Theory, and Job Embeddedness Theory. Additionally, the study identifies several emerging research areas that warrant further scholarly attention, such as predictive analytics, decision trees, random forests, employee engagement, job satisfaction, organizational performance management, and employee turnover. Keywords: Artificial Intelligence, Employee Retention, Human Capital, Social Exchange and Emotional Intelligence

title: Sustainable development goals in the construction industry: science mapping of present and future trends
journal: Built Environment Project and Asset Management
abstract: Purpose Despite increasing attention to the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), the construction industry remains fragmented in its alignment with global sustainability targets. Existing works are dispersed across disciplines, limiting a clear understanding of dominant research themes and emerging directions. Therefore, this study aims to evaluate the knowledge structure and identify future research trends of the SDGs within the construction industry. Design/methodology/approach A bibliometric science mapping analysis was conducted on 1,026 peer-reviewed journal articles published in English and indexed in the web of science (WoS) core collection. Citation analysis, co-citation analysis, and co-word analysis were performed to examine the intellectual structure and future research trends. Findings The citation analysis revealed five themes, the co-citation analysis identified six clusters and the co-word analysis produced six clusters. Additionally, five key themes were identified regarding the knowledge structure of the SDGs within the construction industry: sustainable construction practices, environmental policy and governance, sustainable urban and regional development, innovative technologies for sustainable construction and carbon efficiency in construction. Originality/value This study is original in offering a structured overview of how SDG research has evolved in construction and highlighting future areas that require further investigation.

title: Motor neuropathy following inhalational exposure to aluminum phosphide: a case report and literature review
abstract: Introduction: Aluminum phosphide (AlP) poisoning is a common means of self-harm in South Asia, while accidental inhalational exposure remains underreported despite its severe toxicity. This case reports a unique instance of acute neurotoxicity following inhalational AlP exposure, thereby expanding the current knowledge of phosphine-related neurological sequelae. Case presentation: A previously healthy 21-year-old female developed acute shortness of breath, vomiting, tingling sensations, and symmetrical limb weakness after inhaling AlP fumes. Examination revealed respiratory distress with bilateral limb weakness but preserved consciousness. Investigations showed respiratory alkalosis, elevated lactate, and transient hyperglycemia. She required ICU admission, mechanical ventilation, high-flow oxygen, and supportive care. Although clinically stabilized, persistent bilateral lower-limb weakness remained. Neuroimaging excluded central causes, suggesting peripheral motor neuropathy secondary to phosphine toxicity. Nerve conduction studies were planned during follow-up; however, the patient declined further evaluation because of financial constraints. Conclusion: This case highlights inhalational AlP as a dangerous route of exposure, capable of inducing early and significant neurological deficits even in the absence of altered mental status. It underscores the importance of heightened clinical vigilance, early neuro-monitoring, and research into neuroprotective strategies for phosphine toxicity. It also emphasizes the need for public education on the dangers of pesticide misuse, particularly in unventilated environments.

title: Workplace well-being: the impact on individual and organizational performance
abstract: Purpose This paper aims to review the latest management developments across the globe and pinpoint practical implications from cutting-edge research and case studies. Design/methodology/approach This briefing is prepared by an independent writer who adds their own impartial comments and places the articles in context. Findings Workplace well-being is shown to have a significant and positive effect on both individual and organizational performance. Originality/value The briefing saves busy executives, strategists and researchers hours of reading time by selecting only the very best, most pertinent information and presenting it in a condensed and easy-to-digest format.
